name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) - Pegasus Chapter
relevance: The official WMU chapter of AIAA, providing networking and hands-on engineering experience in aeronautics and astronautics.

name: Advanced Rocketry Club (ARC)
relevance: A project team within AIAA that competes in the NASA Student Launch competition and builds high-powered rockets.

name: Remote Control Aircraft Team (RCAT)
relevance: A sub-team of AIAA focused on designing, building, and flying remote-controlled aircraft and participating in flight competitions.

name: Western Aerospace Launch Initiative (WALI)
relevance: Dedicated to the design, fabrication, and operation of small satellites (CubeSats) and increasing access to space education.

name: Aerospace Laboratory for Plasma Experiments (ALPE)
relevance: Investigates plasma for space propulsion, enhanced combustion, and environmental applications, particularly electric propulsion systems.

name: SPAR Institute (Space Power and Propulsion for Agility, Responsiveness and Resilience)
relevance: A multi-university research institute (WMU partner) focused on developing electric propulsion technologies and space power for the U.S. Space Force.

name: Energy Efficient and Autonomous Vehicles Lab (EEAVL)
relevance: Conducts research on autonomous ground and aerial vehicles (UAVs), focusing on energy efficiency and safety in robotics.

name: Applied Aerodynamics Laboratory
relevance: Houses advanced wind tunnels and focuses on experimental aerodynamics research and flow measurement techniques.

name: Computational Engineering Physics Laboratory (CEPL)
relevance: Focuses on computational research in multidisciplinary engineering physics, including aerospace-related simulations and high-performance computing.

name: Bronco Robotics Club
relevance: A project-based student organization focused on designing, building, and competing with robots in various competitions.

name: Autonomous Vehicles at WMU
relevance: Develops autonomous ground vehicle technology and participates in the Intelligent Ground Vehicle Competition (IGVC).

name: Earth Sciences Remote Sensing (ESRS) Laboratory
relevance: Utilizes satellite data and remote sensing techniques for research in geological and environmental sciences.

name: Sky Broncos
relevance: Western Michigan University's precision flight team that competes in national intercollegiate flying competitions.

name: Organization of Black Aerospace Professionals (OBAP) - WMU Chapter
relevance: Promotes minority representation and professional development in the aerospace and aviation industries.

name: Women in Aviation International (WAI) - WMU Chapter
relevance: Supports and encourages women in aviation and aerospace through networking, scholarships, and professional guidance.

name: Western Aerospace Thin-Sat (WATS) Project
relevance: A student project team that developed and tested plasma-sensing technology on a Thin-Sat platform launched to space.

name: WMU Physics Club
relevance: Promotes physics and astronomy interests through research talks, social activities, and community outreach.

name: Sunseeker Solar Car Project
relevance: Designs and builds solar-powered racing vehicles, integrating renewable energy with advanced controls and mechanical engineering.

name: Kalamazoo Flying Saucer Study Club
relevance: A historical group from the 1950s in Kalamazoo focused on UFO phenomena; affiliation with WMU is uncertain.
